title: Whose money life have I decided isn't allowed to be mine?
subtitle: A journal set for examining the specific people whose financial success you have decided is not available to you.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for Week 3 of the MONEY program. Five prompts for finding the specific financial lives you have excluded yourself from, and examining why.
sourceNotes: Drawn from Day 16 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and Week 3 of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A journal set for Day 16 of the MONEY program: examining the specific financial lives you have decided are not available to you, and tracing where that decision came from. Five prompts, around thirty minutes.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
When I see someone else succeed financially, what is my first internal response?
Write it plainly. Admiration, envy, dismissal, a sense of "of course for them", name what actually happens.
What belief about myself does that response carry?
Follow the response back one step. What does it say about what you believe is available to you?
Whose money life have I decided isn't allowed to be mine?
Name the person or type of person specifically. The one whose income, business, or wealth you admire but have silently decided is not for you.
What would I have to believe about myself to step into that financial life?
Write the belief, not the plan. What would have to be true about you for it to be available?
What is one thing I can do this week that belongs to the version of me who is in that financial life?
One concrete action. Not a transformation, a single choice that the excluded version of you would make.
Drawn from Day 16 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and Week 3 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
